_about: DB-verified keystone counts for the heart-base study, snapshot 194,710 passages (2026-06-19), re-verified against the live dhamma-pg on 2026-06-23. This file is the committed audit record for the load-bearing zeros: build_dataset.py reads it and asserts the dataset literals against it (the consistency gate fails the build on any drift). The page references it as the source of the keystone counts. Per-million-character figures use the authoritative per-layer character totals (mula 53.5M, attha 29.5M, tika 28.4M, anya 9.9M).

by_work_role_stem._note: hadayavatth%/hadayarup% by work_role; mula 18 is the Visuddhimagga only, the Niddesa (anya/kn) and Milindapanha (anya) sit outside the mula role, hence the 20-row root-text total above is layer-by-composition, not the mula work_role count

unnamed_posit._note: the Patthana's yam-rupam-nissaya clause; the concept the name-search can never find. The posit appears across roughly 5-7 Patthana rows by phrasing variant; 7 is the count under the stem search of record.

negative_control._note: cakkhu-vatthu, a matched material support, co-occurs with a verification formula 0 times, exactly like the heart-base; bhavanga, an experienced state, co-occurs 8 times. So 'posited, never verified' partly reflects the grammar of material supports as a class, not a downgrade peculiar to the heart-base.

bhavanga_by_layer._note: the raw ramp partly tracks the ~58M-char commentary bulk against the 53.5M-char canon; normalized per million characters the direction holds but the magnitude shrinks. The signal is genuine: the atthakatha has more rows than the tika (about 92k vs 82k) yet fewer raw hits (235 vs 569), so density climbs independently of row count.
